page.projectNameHint: A unique identifier for your deployment
page.errorInvalidUrlDetails: The repository URL format is not recognized. Please check the URL and try again.
page.errorScanFolderFailed: We couldn't scan this folder. Make sure the path is correct and accessible.
page.errorLoadRepoFailed: We couldn't load this repository. It might be private, doesn't exist, or you don't have access to it.

dns.title: DNS Configuration
dns.addRecordsFor: Add these records at your DNS provider for
dns.descCname: Routes your domain through the edge network
dns.descA: Points your domain to the server
dns.selfInfoMid: pointing to your server IP, then add the

sidebar.cloneTokenNoRepo: No GitHub repository for this deployment.
sidebar.cloneTokenCopied: git clone command copied. The embedded token expires within the hour.
sidebar.cloneTokenFailed: Could not mint a clone token
sidebar.freeDomainFeature: Using free .{domain} domains on your own server
sidebar.freeDomainDesc: Free .{domain} domains are routed through Openship Cloud. To deploy this project to your own server, either connect Openship Cloud or switch this project to a custom domain.
sidebar.freeDomainHint: If you do not want to connect Openship Cloud, change the project domain from free to custom before deploying.
sidebar.servicesFreeDomainFeature: Using free .{domain} domains for your services
sidebar.servicesFreeDomainDesc: One or more exposed services use free .{domain} domains. To deploy them to your own server, either connect Openship Cloud or switch those services to custom domains.
sidebar.servicesFreeDomainHint: Custom domains work without Openship Cloud. Free managed domains do not.
sidebar.unreachableBody: This stack has app services with ports, but none of them are configured with a public domain. If you deploy now, the services can run internally, but users will not be able to access them from a URL.
sidebar.unreachableStep1: Pick the service that should be public.
sidebar.unreachableStep3: Set a subdomain or custom domain and choose the public port.

targetStep.heading: Where do you want to deploy?
targetStep.chooseSubtitle: Choose where your application will run
targetStep.onlyOneSubtitle: Only one deploy target is currently available
targetStep.noTargetSubtitle: No deploy target is available yet
targetStep.noTargetBody: Connect Openship Cloud or add a server to continue with this deployment.
targetStep.saveDefault: Save as my default for every deployment.
targetStep.saveDefaultHint: Change it later in Settings, or per-deploy from the picker on this page.
targetStep.savedToast: Saved as your default deploy target
targetStep.saveFailedToast: Couldn't save default - your deploy will still continue
targetStep.gitForwardDescPre: Clone on the server using your local
targetStep.gitForwardDescPost: identity — no build-and-upload. Nothing is stored on the server.

options.serverViaSsh: Deploy to your remote server via SSH.
options.cloudConnectedDesc: Deploy to managed cloud infrastructure. No server setup needed.
options.cloudDisconnectedDesc: Connect your Openship Cloud account and deploy to managed infrastructure.

hint.cloud: Builds run in managed cloud infrastructure - nothing to set up.
hint.serverNamed: Builds run on your server "{name}". If it's a small VPS, building on this machine can be much faster. You can change this on the next screen or save a preference in Settings.
hint.server: Builds run on your server. If it's a small VPS, building on this machine can be much faster. You can change this on the next screen or save a preference in Settings.
hint.local: Builds run on this machine. You can change this on the next screen or save a preference in Settings.

clone.heading: Clone location
clone.descLead: The build always runs on the server.
clone.serverDesc: Clone on the server. No upload.

power.heading: Power
power.subtitle: Pick a runtime size for Openship Cloud
power.customDesc: Dial in CPU, RAM, and storage yourself
power.modalSubtitle: Pick the CPU, RAM, and disk this deployment should run with.

runtime.heading: Runtime
runtime.subtitle: How it's isolated
runtime.directDesc: Runs on the host. Lowest overhead.
runtime.caveatLowRam: Limited RAM here — Sandboxed adds ~50–80 MB. Direct is lighter, Sandboxed is safer.
runtime.caveat: Direct means full host access if compromised. Sandboxed adds ~1–2% CPU for real isolation.

addServer.title: Add your server
addServer.subtitle: Connect via SSH. You can finish setup later.
addServer.serverNameHint: Optional label shown in the picker.
addServer.agentInfoLead: Connects using this machine's SSH agent — like VSCode, no password or key needed. The host running Openship must already have access to this server (a key loaded in its
addServer.passwordRequiredTest: Password is required to test connection
addServer.keyRequiredTest: Key path is required to test connection

mail.title: Deploy webmail
mail.subtitle: Stand up a self-hosted webmail at the domain you choose.
mail.missingServerBody: Open this page from the mail overview.
mail.deployToHint: Webmail can live on this mail server, on another openship-managed host, or on Opshcloud.
mail.domainHintProxy: Your mail server already owns this hostname - we'll proxy it through your mail VPS to the Opshcloud workload. No DNS changes needed.
mail.domainHintCloud: Point a CNAME at the *.opsh.io URL we provision (you'll see it after deploy).
mail.domainHintDefault: The URL operators will visit. DNS must point at the deploy target.
